title: Classification of Systems
summary: This page explains the decomposition property of linear systems, which allows separation of output into zero-input response (ZIR) and zero-state response (ZSR) components, illustrated with an RC circuit example. It also demonstrates that linearity requires both ZIR and ZSR to obey superposition with respect to their respective causes. Example 1.10 proves that a first-order constant-coefficient linear differential equation describes a linear system by showing the system satisfies the superposition principle.
